Sorry, I'm used to Google Meet.

To your point about looking at different channels, we do have the medium filter here.
So you can...
So it for your entire website, or if you want to cohort it based on growthx versus non-growthx URLs, you can do that as well.
So this should give you that.

We have engaged sessions, and to your point about the bounce rate, Google sort of moved away from, when they switched from UA to GA4, they moved away from bounce rate and towards the engaged sessions metric.
It's really just the other side of the coin, of the bounce rate.
So an engaged session is when someone spends at least a minute on your page, or clicks onto at least one other URL within that section.

So the engagement rate over here that we're showing, this is kind of, it's a, I won't say it's a replacement, but it kind of does a better job of measuring how engaged people are on your page compared to the bounce rate.
We can still add the bounce rate to this table over here for each page.
9% engagement for your homepage, basically, you know, 56 is, you know, the, basically, the bounce.
Something I want to point out, in case you're interested in fine-tuning the engagement rate, you can customize it in GA4.
I don't think that's a beat right now, but just mentioning it.
So, okay, so I guess if it's page part, we can, I'll get to page parts shortly.
But basically, just want to point out, like, on all of your pages here, you can filter by the medium.
So that's there, and then we have a bounce rate equivalent.
So I think that sort of handles it, if I'm hearing you correctly.
Regarding conversions, we do have events.

So we are measuring this out of the box.
Is there something else you wanted to add on top of this?
In terms of conversions?
More so a clarifying event.
was wondering, so one of our conversion rate events is called GA event.

Okay.

I'm always

just wanted to pick your brain because I haven't found a solution that works super well for us either.

It's not super sophisticated.

Okay.
Well, thank you for letting me pick your brain at least a good bit.

Durations, we can add it to this table over here.
So you get like the average session duration, et cetera.
So that shouldn't be an issue.
Then user journeys.
So that is a bit problematic again in Luca, kind of like the events that we just spoke about.
So GA4, I know the exploration is a little difficult to set up.
You kind of have to define the starting and the ending journey, and then it makes sense.
And if I'm correct, it doesn't show you every single page that it hits along the way.
Like, Like, unless you can't.
It clicking on the exploration, and then it further shows you which page it's gone to, etc.
It's kind of the same in Looker.
There's not much of a difference.
The easiest way to show a journey in Looker is if you can define the starting page and if you can define the ending page.
Then what it will do is it will show you the percentage of users who are going to, who say landed on page one or the starting page, and how many of them went to page A versus page B, and so on.
It's basically a two-step kind of a chart.
There is no multi-step chart as there is in GA4 explorations.
So that's the state of things in Looker right now.
Thanks for clarifying that.
Well, I think that kind of answers a lot of my questions, Paul.
Do you have anything else?
No.
Anything you'd like to add?

If you can export it as a CSV or as a sheet, we can create a separate tab for just that data, and we could display that or visualize it however you wish to do so.
Okay, cool.

was kind of thinking the same thing about adding maybe just a separate tab to visualize the qualified.

leave.

problem.

I'm not able to publish because of that weird glitch.
So we'll keep you posted on that.
Hopefully, by the end of the day, that's
It's all resolved and we can go ahead and publish everything that's in the backlog.
Okay.
Okay.
Perfect.
Yeah.
I don't think our publishers mentioned anything, but craft is a little bit of a tricky CMS.
I do have to say it's like not very intuitive.

We call her a diva.
Yeah.

Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.

And then all of the ones, the existing ones glitch as well.
And it's weird happening only on the existing pages, right?
Maria, every time we've created.

Yeah.

Okay, okay, great.
All right, perfect.
So I know that your team has left, like the revenue optimization piece, or I think, no, revenue forecasting, that one got a significant overhaul.
Did you get a chance to, like, take a look at V2?
Did you have any other notes regarding that piece?
I haven't yet, but I was going to plan on doing that today.
And then also ask Jeremy to take a second pass in terms of viewing, because I know a lot of the feedback came from him.
So I just wanted to check if he could take a look on it.
And then if everything's good to go, I think we're, we can move forward.
But Maria, did you get a chance to see the second version?
I just put in my comments, I think, yesterday, and I believe Matt responded that he was gonna address them and send us a V2.
So, yeah.
I can definitely take a look at that when that's ready.
Okay.
Okay.
Perfect.
All right.
So I have some scrunch updates for you as well.
So scrunch is constantly being updated.
So the latest update is that it allows you to look at historical data, I think over the past 12 weeks, which previously was wasn't possible.
So now we can kind of go back in time.
Yeah, this is the last 12 weeks.
So it allows us to go back in time by 12 weeks.
And that has kind of like shifted the reporting again a little bit.

So if we look at like the last 12 weeks, like your overall competitive, like your the last seven days versus the last 90 days, your performance, like as invisibility has increased by like roughly up 1%.
And then if we go to prompts over the last 12 weeks, if we're like comparing it, like
You're in the red for like most of these topics.

I mean, the account-based marketing one and sales pipeline management are the ones where you're seeing some growth and visibility, but most of these are kind of being captured by competitors, primarily Gong and Sales Loft from what I'm seeing in this column right here.
If we change the data to like the last four weeks, it's all like very much the same.
So it's very, very similarly impacted in terms of visibility.
So that's where we're at right now with AI visibility.
Let me just check the last seven days to see if there's been, okay, so last seven days, there's been an improvement.
Considerably.
So if this continues trending up, then hopefully, like the overall, like month over month, we're going to continue seeing growth.
But this is just scrunches being sometimes like it's the updates to the UI and like just the way that they track things.
It's a little challenging to like compare things week over week because they've implemented these changes.
But overall, like the weekly performance looks great.
But if we're like looking at the last 12 weeks, it doesn't look good.
So I'm not sure, like, do you still do you like want me to export the data from like just the week to like the week to week?
Or do you want to see just a lot like the total 12 weeks?
Yeah, I think the week to week is good for us to kind of keep it fresh and make sure and like we'll have some sort of assessment in that sense.
It's good that we're able to see them.
Historical data, though, because it also shows, like, where we were kind of needing some adjustments with our strategy and where we're going.
But I think overall having a week-to-week basis of showcasing where we are, I think, will help align even further, too.
And I wanted to thank you as well for providing that context when you shared those screenshots with us, because I did share that with the broader team.

And I'm planning on meeting with our PR team to kind of align a little bit more on how we can amplify outreach to AI agents' capability externally, you know, through the press releases and thought leadership and whatnot, media coverage.

And I'm also chatting with our team who handles the relationship with G2 and Trust Radius to think through how we can also make sure we're showcasing our AI agents' capabilities further there, because they are pretty new, and we've just launched these pages.
So I think we'll start seeing a better outcome in terms of our impact in the future.
But being able to see, like, the last seven days at least gives us...
It's like a picture of what's going on and helps us kind of know what we need to do.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
And, you know, what's interesting is that like the citations or, you know, like the brand citations just aren't like your website isn't being cited, but neither are your competitors.
Like it's all just third party sites.
So like for 90, like 97% of these prompts, the responses include like citations from other websites that aren't yours.
And that kind of goes back to like the PR thing and just like making sure that it's mentioned in the right places and read it as well.
So it's like it's important like to publish content on your website, you know, to inform the LLMs.
But when it comes to citations, it's like important to cast a wide net.
So I'm happy to hear.
Y'all are doing that internally.
Yeah, yeah.

I was about to ask you from, based on like you handling other clients and other industries, is this common to see or is it just our industry that's really showcasing more of the citations being pulled from third party instead of the actual websites?
This is something that's happening across the board.
So it's something that people have been seeing like across industries that LLMs have been like citations are mostly come from like third party websites.
They come from, it really depends on what the prompt is, but like if you're looking for like product reviews, LLMs defer to like review platforms.
Makes sense.
Yeah, like if you're looking for customer experiences, they're going to pull reviews.
So they do, it's not something that's just related to like sales or revenue.
Yeah.
It kind of falls if I were to take it into the line.
Like traditional SEO concept.
It's like all about like the backlinks basically, right?
Like building that, like building that backlink portfolio and whatnot.
So that makes sense.
Okay.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
All right.
Did you have any other questions that you wanted me to help with today?
I think so.
We're all good on the content side.
You should be getting new content soon.
And I'll let Matt know that we can go ahead and stage the content that's already kind of been reviewed.
Okay.
No, yeah.
On my end, I'm good.
Maria, anything on your end?
I think I'm all set too.
I'm going through today and reviewing a few more of the new ones for the week.
And yeah, everything is looking really great.
And thank you so much.
I'm really happy with the process, everything.
Yeah, images too.
Great.
It just looks amazing.
So thank you for that as well.
Oh, yeah.
Thank you.
I'm so happy to hear that.
All right.
Well, I will send you the scrunch like insight snippet in a couple of hours after I get off these calls.
But that'll be ready for you in a little bit as well.
Thank you so much.
appreciate that.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss Looker dashboard enhancements and provide updates on content strategy and AI visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=21.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Looker dashboard improvements discussed, including filtering by medium, conversion events, and potential custom event creation for more accurate conversion tracking](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=369.0)
  - [Content updates in progress, with new blogs and refreshes being edited and staged](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [Scrunch tool updated to show 12-week historical data, revealing mixed AI visibility results across different timeframes](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1337.0)
  - [Team to focus on amplifying Outreach's AI capabilities through PR, thought leadership, and review platforms](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1516.0)

## Topics

### Looker Dashboard Enhancements

  - [Current dashboard includes medium filtering, engaged sessions metric (replacing bounce rate), and conversion events](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=407.0)
  - [Potential improvements:](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=369.0)
      - [Add views and average session duration to existing charts](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=807.0)
      - [Create custom events for more accurate conversion tracking from Qualified chatbot data](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
      - [Explore options for visualizing user journeys (limited in Looker compared to GA4)](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=813.0)
  - [Possibility of adding a separate tab for Qualified chat data if exported as CSV/sheet](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=928.0)

### Content Updates and CMS Issues

  - [New blogs and refreshes being edited, team deciding on staging process](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [CTA box glitch in Craft CMS affecting refresh publications](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1174.0)
      - [Issue only occurring on refreshes, not new content](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1251.0)
      - [Team working with Andrew to resolve the problem](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1174.0)

### Scrunch AI Visibility Updates

  - [New feature: 12-week historical data view](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1337.0)
  - [Mixed results across different timeframes:](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1372.0)
      - [Last 12 weeks: 1% increase in visibility, but red for most topics](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1372.0)
      - [Last 7 days: Considerable improvement in visibility](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1431.0)
  - [Competitors (primarily Gong and Sales Loft) capturing visibility for most topics](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1372.0)
  - [Citations predominantly coming from third-party sites (97% of prompts), not company websites](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1566.0)

### Content Strategy and External Amplification

  - [Team planning to meet with PR team to align on amplifying Outreach's AI capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1516.0)
  - [Exploring opportunities with G2 and Trust Radius to showcase AI agents' capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1537.0)
  - [Focus on increasing brand citations across various platforms, including Reddit](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1603.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Hadassah to review V2 of revenue forecasting piece and involve Jeremy for feedback](https://fathom.video/share/xzt8kn1ZTWS4zzs-X2xc3QtJ33QLMDYX?tab=summary&timestamp=1293.0)
